title: Success Criteria, Baselines, and Prompt Evals · Evaluation Brief: Task Contract
summary: Define and test a support-triage prompt before optimizing its wording. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt task contract increment in a versioned prompt evaluation harness. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define the user, task, decision, acceptable output, and material failure modes before drafting a prompt.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

theory case — Use a changed theory case for prompt success evals that tests this competency: defend the module contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Prompt quality can be judged without a defined task outcome. This fails the Task Contract contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Task Contract evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-task-contract-1qejid" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Task Contract into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-task-contract-1qejid
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

options.text: The Task Contract record says a versioned prompt evaluation harness looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve inspect task contract evidence.

The record names the assumption, applies Task Contract, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

stimulus.title: Task Contract failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Prompt quality can be judged without a defined task outcome.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define the user, task, decision, acceptable output, and material failure modes before drafting a prompt.

options.text: For repair the task contract defect, apply Task Contract without its stated preconditions, omit the a versioned prompt evaluation harness trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Task Contract for a versioned prompt evaluation harness.
Reconstruct the conditions for Task Contract, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the Task Contract artifact unchanged and rewrite its explanation so the unsupported output appears intentional. This does not resolve repair the task contract defect.
